This is the headset you want for Xbox One (as of 8/27/15) After two lousy experiences with Turtle Beach, I bit the bullet and bought these. I'm shocked by how much better this unit is than the others.

I had a Turtle Beach 800X on my original Xbox and it was a love/hate relationship. Much of the hate was that it was tied to a massive base station for charging and that it completely failed me (even with purchased converters) when I tried to move it to the Xbox One.


That stopped once I bought the "Elite 800" for the PS4.

What I like:
- Construction
- Battery life
- Separate controls for chat and game audio

What I don't like:
- Constant pops and crackles in the audio.
- VERY loud annoying noise when it 'crashes'
- The mic boom could be half as long
- Overpriced

reference_output:
rating 1.0. Its success is also its downfall. Imagine hundreds of hours of playtime and every single object you interacted with throughout that time was in the same place/position you left it when you interacted with it. Well over time the save file becomes bloated allowing glitches, slowness and errors to creep in until you cannot play the game further.

Yes it's cool to fill a house full of plates, armor, weapons, books and all the various other trinkets you can find. What is not cool is that all of your time leveling and collecting leads to the only ending that is inevitable; game breaking slowness/glitches.

So if you want a medieval grand theft auto that will eventually lead to you having to start over or lose everything then this is for you. If you want to log in and see every object you ever interacted with in the place you left it at the expense of it eventually breaking your game play then this is for you.

Also dragons kill quest people, traders/blacksmiths and a main game quest was an npc who was going to sneak my gear into a certain building where a party was being held but when I got to him inside the building he only had my necklace and chest armor out of a whole suit(this was early in the game). I killed him and realized it is healthier if I don't play this game ever again. This game is over rated. If you are the millions who say the other then more power to you. I just hope this is not the new way of developing video games. If so then pretty soon games will be like a blue screen of death.

reference_output:
rating 2.0. Good headphones, AWFUL microphone This headset is being touted as a great bang for the buck and the best budget headset. I don't agree. It's a great pair of headPHONES but the microphone is god awful.

Some issues with the mic are:

- Constant hiss in the background
- Voice volume is very low unless you turn up "Mic gain" (Mic gain is very bad because it makes the static/hiss very loud)
- Sometimes when I listen to Youtube videos (as an example), the Youtube audio would go into the mic (this is not a case where the video was too loud. The signal actually bleeds into the microphone sometimes)
- This microphone in particular picks up annoying breath sounds despite moving it to the side

I gave one to my sister and had her chat with me while we played games. I couldn't stand listening to her with this headset. I also know 2 people who own this headset and they are unfortunately the butt of many mic jokes because they sound so bad.

There are a few things that you could do to somewhat remedy this headset's mic quality.

- People say you could buy a sound card but I'm not really willing to accept that as a reasonable solution.
- Similar to above, there are $10 adapters that converts 3.5mm headphones and microphone jacks to USB. I wouldn't say that is a solution, but $10 makes it sound a little better.
- There is a solution that makes this microphone completely okay though. That would be push-to-talk. It is however, only a solution to people who enjoy using it. I myself hate push-to-talk.

Moving on from the mic, the build quality feels very sturdy and the packaging is amazing. I can't comment too much on the audio as I am not audiophile, but it was a pleasure listening/gaming to. The headset was comfy right from the start, but it did start to hurt both me and my sister after using it for more than an hour.

Again I say, I don't believe this deserves the status it has, but maybe people just don't care how annoying they sound to others.

reference_output: rating 2.0. Functional system, crap controllers.  Consequently, not worth the price. Despite two stars, I kept this system. On a lark, recalling that I read a review about the poor controllers, I replaced them with 2 NES replacement controllers, which fixed the problem.  But this added another 50% to this system price, which is simply too expensive for what it is.  There's no reason to put these crap controllers in here, and nobody in their right mind would expect them to last.  There is a world of difference between actual NES or NES replacement controllers and these.

reference_output:
rating 3.0. Story? Yes. Trophy Hunting? Easy Peasy. Combat? *Shrugs* Environment: OMG Just be over already... So. I got the platinum trophy in this game. My gameplay time was 62 hours. Do I feel like I got my moneys worth with this game? Yes. If I could go back in time, would I skip over this title? Yes. One thing that I feel is the most important to highlight is something I wish I knew when I started. You may have heard that it requires completing it multiple times, which is true, but each time is from a different perspective and make the story so much better because of it. There is a huge warning I want to put out to people, it is near the end of the review, so those who like surprises don't have to read it. Let's get this review started.

Gameplay is vary varied. To a point where it doesn't seem like the game knew what it wants. It's mainly a 3D hack and slash combat exploration game. But there's 2D platforming, 3D platforming. 2.5D platforming. Twin Stick Shooter parts. Parts where you're in a mech suit flying. Parts where you're in the mech suit just normal fighting. None of these styles were terrible, but none were amazing either.

Difficulty is inconsistent. I started off on Normal because Very Hard is a one hit kill with permadeath that I didn't like the idea of. Normal was pretty easy. I didn't die up until the 3rd boss fight. And when I was dying, it wasn't because of skill, it was because the camera would get stuck in a weird position and I couldn't tell if it was by design or happenstance. This is also when I realized I wasn't enjoying the game, so I looked up if any of the trophies were difficulty related, and they aren't, so I put it on easy. And easy can be REALLY easy. There are upgrades you can get that make certain parts of combat run themselves. So you can do auto-evade, and auto fire, and auto attack, and auto weapon switch, and then all you need to do is just go from map marker to map marker, the game can play itself.

Story. It is complicated. I am still unpacking what I just played, and I will need to revisit some of it to fully understand it. This part is important. There are six endings that I got, there might be more. And two endings aren't really endings, but, yea. Take notes while playing.

For Trophy Hunters! You want an easy platinum trophy? Put the game on easy, make sure that you have the Auto chips enabled, and only do the main story missions. Don't get sidetracked by other stuff. Don't sell anything. Only buy the upgrades that let you equip more upgrades.You want to max the drop rate with upgrades. After you get the 4 main endings, there is an NPC in the game that will sell you trophies using in-game currency. Around 2 million in game currency units should be enough, which I had way more of just by playing normally. Do it right, and you can be complete in 10-15 hours.

For this next part, I will be talking about something as vague as I can, but I still feel the need to announce that it could be sonsidered a spoiler, so.

MASSIVE SPOILER AND GENERAL WARNING!

The game will delete your save data if you let it.There is one part after getting one of the endings where you get to watch the credits while playing the hacking mini game, and the game asks you if you want to help out your fellow gamers. It is all pretty straight forward. But the part that I did not know was that it will delete all three of your save game slots, not just the one you make the decision on.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Great case that protects and stores well ! I kept reading this case and another one were the best to get. After getting the runaround from the other one I looked at this one and I found that this had all the same features but it looked better too.

Been using it since christmas and it's a great case. took it on a trip and it worked out very well. Enough room for 4 joycons total, the wrist straps for all four, a samsung galaxy s9 charger and charging cable, the switch itself and if we owned more than one physical game, it would have housed all the game cartridges too. Nothing felt cramped or compromising, it really felt like a solid unit.

I want to point out though that the form-fitting indentations for the switch + attached joycons makes it nigh impossible to have any sort of switch body protector applied and stored with it in the case, which is massively disappointing, but you get trade-offs when you don't want to carry around a huge and bulky case.

I also wish a shoulder strap was an option, maybe hooks for it or something. sometimes it's a bit of a pain to only have the hand strap

Review 1: rating 5.0. Ok for 11 year old in my opinion. Bought this for my 11 year old son. Wanted something a little more interesting than some of the games for his age, but was worried about language and violence. I've watched him play quite a bit now and I can say that the game is not too violent and does not have foul language (at least I haven't heard any). Now, before someone attacks my review over the definition of 'too violent' let me be clear that there is a lot of shooting and such in the game.... Review 2: rating 4.0. A mix of Reach and Halo 3 for me I've always enjoyed the Halo series, my first one I ever bonded with though was Halo 3 (my childhood!). Reach was different, it was a good game, but it didnt put off the vie I got from playing Halo 3. Halo 4 is a blend of the new stuff from Reach, and the same good ol' feeling from Halo 3. Old dog, new tricks. Definitely would recommend! Review 3: rating 5.0. 343 did an amazing job 343 had some shoes to fill to appease the Halo fanatics and in my honest opinion as a halo fan since original xbox days I was not disappointed. reference_output:
rating 4.0. however it is still comfortable overall. Appearance Comfort: 4/5 the ear muffs have some firmness and I’ve used softer, however it is still comfortable overall.
Appearance: 5/5 looks nice and sleek. I’m not into headphones lighting up and having the bells and whistles, if you are these might be a bit bland for you.
Controls: 3/5 most of the controls are on the left ear phone. Takes a bit to get used to where each button is without having to look at it. I think they should have put a small bump maybe on the volume up or the chat/ game audio to make it a bit easier to remember what your turning up. The other issues is these buttons are right by the mic so sometimes people here my hands running right across the mic when I’m adjusting the sound. Last, it does use a wireless usb to connect.
Sound 4.5/5 the sound is great, I love using the mix as well through the PlayStation app which allows you to create your own as well (I recommend maxing the bass, having the mid high and a lower treble for shooters, the bullet noises can be ear piercing if the treble is too high). Lastly an issue is that people hear there voice slightly echo since the ear piece is so close to the mic. Overall I would recommend them, If you can get over a couple of the flaws I mentioned they are worth it!

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Older one works great I purchased one around 2012 and it has worked flawlessly. It is $20 for ease of use and very minor quality improvements.

Primary benefit: The wii is now easy to move between modern hdmi ready TVs.

Secondary benefit: consistent quality. I honestly didn't expect much since the output is still Ntsc. After buying this, I noticed lots of video and audio artifacts from the a/v cable signal (hdmi route yields slighly better audio range, the dirty power related flickering is gone and colors appeared modestly richer). While hdmi adapter does a fairly clean 720 upscale from ntsc, there is a slight loss of sharpness if you look closely. All in all the Hulu / Amazon prime kids shows look better with the hdmi adapter.

Most of the negative reviews regarding poor product quality seem recent. No comment. The 2012 version still works for us.

Review 1: rating 1.0. Comically horrible mic, awful software I was surprised by just how bad the quality of this headset really is. The packaging makes it look like a high-quality product and the headset is comfortable enough, but the audio quality and the mic make a fast food drive through sound like an upgrade. The audio of the headset is decent enough, but once you install the software that comes with it, everything you listen to sounds like it's in a bathtub, with tons of reverb and a huge loss in cri... Review 2: rating 2.0. Eh. The mic being detachable means that most computers won't recognize it without you making so all inputs are separate. However, the only way to do this is with Realtek which I don't have. They were comfortable to wear, however. Review 3: rating 5.0. Great sound quality I really like these! My partner has the same headset and told me about them. He said he can hear me far better now when we are playing Apex Legends. And if I play solo missions or play with random people, having the mic mute button is a great addition. You will not be sorry owning these. Review 4: rating 2.0. P!ss poor Headset. You know for a headset to be a headset the mic + software needs to be good. That is not the case. The USB sound card it comes with really sucks. I mean hard. Mic sound quality is trash. Horrible fidelity. For comparison I used a Yeit Pro, Razor Headset, and a $10 mics from my junk drawer. The $10 dollar mic whooped this "headset" into paste. Which is a shame. This is THE MOST COMFORTABLE headset I've ever worn. Sound quality is good, not great. I have a pair of Sku... Review 5: rating 1.0. Get another headset mic doesn’t last long. Lasted 2 months. And stopped working. Horrible headset. Mic didn’t last even a month. Last razer headset I will ever buy….. you lost a customer. Review 6: rating 2.0. Crap Well I opened it and used it right away. Well the mic kept coming off and the mute button did not work. I tried it on a ps4 console. I played with it and it didn't work at all. The sound isn't great, I have a pair that are some off brand that is better than these. I expected better since they cost more and had a mute button. I'm going to return them. Review 7: rating 1.0. Used more like Abused One of the few times I've trusted Amazon with used items, spoiler alert major disappointment followed. Headset was okay at best probably the best budget on the market but spring for a higher end. Review 8: rating 2.0. Clicking Noise from Hell For a $90 pair of headphones, you figure your getting a good set. Unfortunately not the case with mine. The sound is great and they're comfortable but the left ear cup has a clicking noise FROM HELL. It's just audible enough to drive me crazy considering there isn't great noise cancellation. I can literally hear my overhead fan with these things on. The noise is a huge dealbreaker as nothing gets rid of it and it just clicks whenever you slightly move yo... Review 9: rating 1.0. Super low volume on PS4 even at max volume makes them useless for PS4 I just received a brand new Razor Blackshark V2, and the volume on the PS4 is pathetically low! I have tried it with both the 3.5mm and the USB inputs. The headphone volume on the PS4 is at max volume and the volume knob on the ear is turned all the way up. Even with everything at max level, it's still not high enough. I'm not hard of hearing, and I don't listen to everything at super high levels. Hell, even my fiv... Review 10: rating 2.0. Broke within a week Microphone stopped working within a week. Review 11: rating 2.0. Voice chat quality is awful I’ll keep it short. People I play with say the voice quality is horrendous and sounds cheap. Overall game volume is decent but not spectacular. There is only one knob to adjust overall volume instead of one for chat and game. Not compatible with other devices for good quality. Get a different headset. Review 12: rating 5.0. Better than my astro a50s The sound on these headphones are the best I've ever owned. I had a pair of ASTRO a50 headphones I paid 300$ for and these 80$ headphones crushes them. Dont hesitate if you want a pair of really good gaming headphones buy these, you won't regret it. Bravo razor!! Review 13: rating 1.0. Sound great - when they work I ordered 3 pair of gaming headphones to compare - the Razer BlackShark V2, HyperX Cloud Core and Corsair HS50 Pro.

The HyperX was pretty good, they were very light and the 7.1 surround sound was good but the ear pads didn't extend far enough down to be comfortable for long. The Corsairs were too heavy and the headband was tight.

The Razers were by far the best. Extremely light and comfortable, the mic quality is better than the Hype... Review 14: rating 4.0. Great product. I love em Really great pair of headphones. I’ve only ever used turtle beaches and a cheap $25 Chinese pair from Amazon, my latest pair of turtle beaches was unreliable with sound and would cut out often when I was playing certain Games, and having the charge the headphones even though they were wired was absurd. After about a year they snapped after being dropped, even thought I took great care of them and never abused them. So I decided I had had enough and wanted to ... Review 15: rating 1.0. Sound cuts in/out Unfortunatley, these are less than a year old, and sound quality is horrible. Started off great, but now nothing but cutting in and out. Review 16: rating 2.0. Good for YouTube. Thats about it. I like to consider myself to be at least a little tech-savvy. I know my general way around technology. That is not the case for this.

TL;DR at the bottom.

Here are the pros:
As a physical object, its phenomenal. Its lightweight, comfortable, and doesn't put very much pressure on my glasses. In this regard, it is better than my HyperX Cloud 2.

The audio is good... If all you're doing is watching YouTube. Listenin... Review 17: rating 5.0. Great sound, an absolutely must have [[VIDEOID:bf4aab95dd01905b3eff60d711301722]] Best headset i own. I just wish the cable didnt coil up as much. But its excellent for games like hunt showdown which audio is the key to victory. Review 18: rating 4.0. Wish the 3.5mm was louder They're nice. They feel light on the head and cover my (smaller) ears comfortably. They have a good feel and when paired with the software on PC, they sound great! The ear cups insulate really well and there's very little bleed out into the room. I happen to really like the volume knob on the side of the headset - no searching for buttons or hitting the wrong one on accident.

However... and this may just be a "me" problem, but when they're plugged... Review 19: rating 2.0. hot, sweaty and gripped my head too tightly decent sound but found the earcups very uncomfortable Review 20: rating 5.0. Very good I am absolutely impressed with these. I had turtle Beach wireless set I gave over a hundred for and they would cut out and pop crack with certain audio. These sound great and don't make your ears sweat.
persona: This customer values functional performance and practicality in accessories, prioritizing reliable operation and compatibility, but is frustrated by design flaws such as non-detachable cords and ill-fitting charging components, indicating a preference for well-engineered, user-friendly products that minimize the risk of damage or improper use.
reference_output: rating 2.0. Wire stopped working in only 6 months it was great while it lasted, its ashame they still use undetachable cords to these headphones

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Five Stars Best ps4 game to date Review 2: rating 4.0. Leave the damn Hinterlands! Also, hang out with Iron Bull, Dorian, and Sera whenever you can. That's all you need to know to enjoy this game. Review 3: rating 5.0. great game! Has a few glitches but dosent affect ... great game! Has a few glitches but dosent affect game play. The strategic part of the game that really pays for itself. Love this game and have played it twice now. Review 4: rating 1.0. One Star No the game doesn't work I need to return it. Review 5: rating 5.0. Get this!!! Fantastic game not one complaint. So many choices, so much content, so much fun. Never gets boring. Could play this for a year and not see all it has to offer. The environments are breathtaking. The game world offers so much to absorb. Defiantly a game other developers should use as a benchmark. Play this game! Review 6: rating 5.0. Great game! Tons of side quests Great game! Tons of side quests. Beautiful graphics. If you like previous Dragon Age or Skyrim, you will love this game. Review 7: rating 4.0. "Dragon age Inquisition" Review! My point of view as a newcomer to the series. Let me start off by this declaimer, I have never played any of the previous dragon age games, and I didn't do that decision thing they did for the previous games (Dragon age keep) so basically I went into this Brand new.

I love RPG's and I on the aftermath of hearing that the "Wither 3" got delayed again I decided to try "Dragon Age Inquisition" after seeing all the positive revi... Review 8: rating 5.0. love it. I've easily poured over 100 hours into DAI since buying it for a Valentines Day gift. Some things are buggy, but it is a lot of fun. 100X better than DA2 and almost better than DAO. It's enormous, expansive, and when you think it's over, there is more to do. Your cast of friends and allies are fun, fleshed out, and you really care about them. You end up having to make some really tough decisions that have profound effects on your gameplay and sometimes all you want to do is ... Review 9: rating 2.0. Disappointing As a big fantasy guy, I thought I'd love this game. It's got too much depth for the amount of gameplay (and repetitiveness) that you get. Unless you read the entire storyline, which would take longer than playing the game itself, you're not going to enjoy it. Review 10: rating 5.0. Life, it becomes it. Dragon Age: Inquisition is an amazing game. The scope of this game isn't realized until 10 to 20 hours in. The graphics are amazing (I have the PS4 version).

Gameplay is very well done. The tactical camera can be a bit unfriendly but that is easy to deal with.

If you love RPGs I highly recommend this game.

That being said...if you are looking for a Multiplayer this game is SERIOUSLY lacking for you. The multi is fun but gets old ... Review 11: rating 5.0. Five Stars excellent game I did not expect it to be this good! Recommend Review 12: rating 3.0. An almost return-to-form for Bioware Dragon Age: Inquisition is a welcome, albeit stumbling, return-to-form of Bioware's greatness. It falls a star short of being a truly excellent games, but it does many things quite well. I have played the game for about 20 hours now. I'll break down my review of each segment of the game in a pro/con style.

Maps--

Pros: The world is expansive. Gone are the linear, copy-and-paste corridors of Dragon Age 2. It has been sai... Review 13: rating 4.0. Great game, but it requires a lot of time The game is a ton of fun, but be ready to invest a lot of your time into this game. I have probably played about 40+ hours and I am not even close to finishing the story.
As far as Dragon Age goes, this game is my favorite in the series. Review 14: rating 5.0. DA:I truly lives up to expectations and beyond!! Origins was a fantastic game. I felt the second Dragon Age felt way short of expectations. I am glad that Inquisition picked up and far exceeded my expectations. This is an amazing game that I could recommend to any gamer, not just RPG fans. Review 15: rating 5.0. Not a beginner's RPG...but I love it! This. Game. Received as a gift for Christmas and I haven't stopped playing after four days. So much to do! So many people to talk to! I haven't even killed a dragon yet!

This is my first experience with a Dragon Age game, and I'm surprised to find that I'm following the story and characters without any prior knowledge. This RPG is not for the faint of heart. While you can blaze through the main story without touching most of the conten... Review 16: rating 5.0. Worth every penny Best game for the ps4 released in 2014. I have put almost 80 hours into this game and still feel as though I'm only half way done. It's great to have a game that I feel is finally giving me my moneys worth for the nex gen console. If you're into RPG's this is a game for you! Review 17: rating 3.0. Maybe I've lost my touch with video games but don't ... Maybe I've lost my touch with video games but don't care for being able to kill enemies with relative ease and then run 20 yards and you whole team gets slaughtered in 10 seconds. Review 18: rating 5.0. Five Stars excellent game, took a while to get accustomed to the controls, but still fun. Review 19: rating 5.0. You wont be able to put it down Do NOT buy if your wife and/or family have problems with you playing video games. You wont be able to put it down. Simple stuff like gathering materials, pelts and plants is weirdly addictive.

This is the best Dragon Age game to the day, and one of the best western RPGs to come to a console. The graphics are beautiful on PS4, the music is fantastic, the combat is really fun and the world is huge! Review 20: rating 5.0. Fantastic RPG game!!! Fantastic RPG game!!! This will put Destiny on the shelf for months to come.

persona: This customer prioritizes high performance, precise responsiveness, and seamless software integration when purchasing tech products, especially gaming peripherals, and is willing to invest in premium features despite brand skepticism, valuing functionality and immersive experience over price.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Great mouse so far I've been using the DeathAdder for years and have loved it...with one exception. The rubber bumpers on the side, where your thumb and middle two fingers sit, have started to become unglued and have come off. After buying this mouse, I see that Razer has tried to fix that, by making the entire sides of the mouse rubberized. The mouse is incredibly fast using the 2.4 Ghz connection and doesn't seem any slower compared to my previous corded mouse. The only hiccup was downloading the latest version of Synapse to customize the mouse. I think some kind of conflict was happening with my old version and I had to reinstall everything and then install Synapse 3. After that, things were working great. Synapse is an amazing software that allows you to customize each button exactly how you want things. I also like that you can switch profiles through a button on the bottom of the mouse and don't have to do it through software. You can load up to five profiles onto the onboard memory. Great job, Razer. You're paying attention to what works and making the improvements. That's how you keep customers.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Way nicer than the photos and makes game racing a blast! Given how much price gouging has been going on lately for gaming gear, I was pleasantly surprised the NLR GT Lite was back to a reasonable price. I was also very pleased on how well designed and manufactured this chair is.
PROS:
- Love the swing-out steering wheel mount for easy entry/exit.
- Easy to fold/stow even with the racing gear attached.
- Excellent packaging, double boxed!
- The chair fabric is like faux ultra-suede, super soft yet very strong.
- NLR gets extra points for a very high quality instruction manual with easy links to assembly videos that made the whole experience of setting this up a pleasure.
- Once set up with the G29 steering system, it was solid even during energetic racing!
- We added a little office-chair-padding to the seat bottom for comfort.
- Great, rapid response from the support team.
Cons:
- A few of the screw holes were super tight and took several attempts to get them to go through.
- I have a standard height PC desk and this chair (even with the highest settings) was a bit too low to the ground. I'm exploring some options with the very responsive support staff at Next Level Racing.

Review 1: rating 5.0. SO much fun!! My 6 year old daughter LOVES iSpy. We had the computer version of Spooky Mansion, and it was her favorite game. She played it all the way through twice.

Anyway, when I saw this title, I wanted to give it a shot, but I was a bit concerned that it would be much like the computer game. I needn't have worried. The little skeleton host was the same, and the general goal of the game is the same, but the puzzles are all different, and while some of the rooms are ... Review 2: rating 5.0. I Spy Spooky Mansion is GREAT! I purchased the I Spy Spooky Mansion for Wii for my 9 year old grandson for Christmas. Needless to say, he was ecstatic! He has the older Spooky Mansion for PC, but this one is more suited for his age group. He is quite savvy with Wii games and is sometimes challenged to find items on the Wii version. It's a great game & he is having a good time playing it! Review 3: rating 4.0. Another Fun I Spy Game I bought this for my four-year-old's birthday after he had mastered Ultimate I Spy, and he loved this game. He's beaten it a few times, but keeps going back to run through it. The game is not as scary as I originally feared, which was a relief. He couldn't put this game down, and likes it as much as the original I Spy Wii game.

The only downside is that it's a bit short, but it's still longer than we initially thought. There are enough minigames to m... Review 4: rating 4.0. I Spy Spooky Mansion I was looking for a present for my nephews 7th birthday. He loves his WII games. So I looked for a game. When i saw "I Spy Spooky Mansion" by Scholastic Games I thought it would be fun. I was plesently surprised when my nephew played the game the first time. Not only was there no violence he had to read and he really had to think to figure out the clues for the hidden objects. He was so used to just shaking the controller and pushing buttons to play a game. Nice ... Review 5: rating 4.0. LOVE I Spy games Sincerely enjoyed the game. My boyfriend sits and plays them with me and we have a lot of fun. Wish they made more of these for WII. Review 6: rating 5.0. Fun Game Great family fun, however the entire game can be completed in one sitting. Since the game was bought used, there was one technical difficulty with it....at certain points the game "froze" and had to be restarted. Fortunately, it restarted from the level we had progressed to, and didnt have to replay the entire game. Review 7: rating 5.0. Great game for the kids (and adults) The I Spy series of games is great for kids and adults. I grew up with the brand and it crossing generations now on the Wii. We hope that some new themes come out as we enjoy finding the clues and searching for items as a family. Good for working together which is sometimes difficult when many games are for 2 players only nowadays. Definitely recommended. Review 8: rating 4.0. Nostalgia This game was just as I remembered it and the 3-D type graphics that the Wii added to it made it even better than the pc version I played as a kid. Not only did I enjoy replaying it again as a college student but it was still a bit challenging at points with things being hidden in the background instead of just in the scene you're engaged in. That said I also like that in this version a lot of the search scenes you have 360 degree control so you can pan around it to look. T... Review 9: rating 4.0. there's more to do than you expect This is a really fun game. I gave it 4 stars instead of 5 because as you solve puzzles and collect clues, the game keeps saying, "you're almost done" , "this is the final clue" etc. only to find out you have to go through all the rooms and solve new puzzles many, many times. Review 10: rating 4.0. Fun Fun game with the kids. They and I were entertained throughout. Some of the items were a little small and a touch more zoom would have been nice but did not slow gameplay. I do recommend it if you like the books. Review 11: rating 5.0. Great game! My 40+ year old daughter and I (70 yr. old) played this WII game and couldn't go to bed because we got so involved in it trying to find the objects! It keeps you on your toes going from room to room in the Spooky Mansion trying to find every thing so that you can escape the mansion. Great game! Review 12: rating 5.0. We love this game We love this game. Even though it is an older game we still play it. Its easy enough for a younger child to play it, but interesting enough for parents to play along as well. We really enjoy the mystery to it as we play along. The children like being on the lookout for each hidden object. Sometimes there are more difficult areas for the kids to do on their own. Parents must play along and help with this or it could get very frustrating for young kids. Overall, I rea... Review 13: rating 3.0. Okay if you like repeating the same thing over and over again. I play this with my 10 y/o we love it, but too have to keep going back over and over through the same house for the next challenges gets old fast. would be nice to have additional rooms or building to explore. Review 14: rating 5.0. Five Stars We all enjoyed this game. Wish they had others Review 15: rating 4.0. Definitely Fun Love this game! It really gets you thinking and is challenging at times. Once you’ve completed it, it becomes a nice distraction to go through it again. Definitely addictive in a good way. Wish you could unlock new puzzles though. By the third completion you know it’s time to set it aside for a while to forget where the objects are. Review 16: rating 5.0. I Spy Spooky Mansion I purchased I Spy Spooky Mansion for my nieces and we all played together. There are several rooms with clues where you need to find the listed items. You need the Wii remote connected to a nunchuck to navigate the rooms. Some clues require a bit of thinking (“bat” might mean the animal or a baseball bat). There are also little mini games built in. You can save your progress. I think it’s a great Wii game for anyone that enjoys I Spy type games.

reference_output: rating 5.0. A Great Family Halloween Game!!! Just in time for Halloween! This game arrived quickly! This game was not an expensive price and very affordable price. I stumbled upon this game when I was searching for Wii games. My daughter and I love this game. We love I Spy games!!! All the graphics and different games to find the pieces of the puzzle were great. We liked how you can go into the different rooms of the haunted house. Great game to play especially now that we are home more because of COVID. I would recommend you to buy this game. This is a great game to play with your family!!

DOOM for Switch is an excellent port! Fantastic all around. Seriously.

The game runs well docked or in handheld mode. The only complaint I have is the sound will occasionally get really quiet and won’t go back up until you reload the game to a checkpoint. Kind of annoying but should be able to be fixed in an update.

As for the game it is good. The game play... Review 10: rating 4.0. The first great FPS on Switch Doom on the Nintendo Switch is an interesting experiment. You are taking a graphically intensive shooter from 2016 and porting it to a console that really shouldn't be able to run it, but it does! Don't go in expecting a pretty game, however, because many technical compromises had to be made to get it on the Switch. reference_output:
rating 5.0. Extremely comfortable for smaller people These are my new favorite gaming headphones!

Style - 5/5 : I personally LOVE the all black color with the small trim of rainbow lights. They look so slick! These headphones are large for my head but less bulky compared to most gaming headsets out there. The microphone swivels up and down easily and doesn't feel too loose or too tight. The wire connecting the headset to the computer is corded and has a nice weight to it. The headset itself is pretty light weight but doesn't feel cheap at all (in my opinion).

Comfort - 5/5 : I can wear this headset for hours without getting a headache. The padding is thick and extremely comfy. I can wear these over my glasses without them smashing my glasses into the sides of my face. My head is also on the smaller side and these are neither too tight nor too loose for my head. This is the most comfortable gaming headset that I own

Sound - 4/5 : After reading other reviews I tried playing around with the settings of my volume in game and then the sound was great. The mic is really responsive and people online can hear me very clearly. The only reason why I'd knock it down a point is because it's advertised to be noise cancelling. These aren't completely noise cancelling, but they do muffle out a lot of outside noise.

Quality for price - 5/5 : Overall I would say this headset is 100% worth it for the price. I wish I had found these a long time ago, would have saved me a chunk of cash that was spent on crappier headsets. These are definitely the best gaming headphones that I own that are under $30!

reference_output:
rating 3.0. The best wireless option today VR is great, i've been doing it since 2016. I got the first Quest when it came out then the second when it came out. VR is much more than gaming. It's just as much educational and social as it is gaming. I'm old, so to watch video games go from Pong to the VR of today was an incredible journey.

Pros,
1) It's wireless VR, amazing, great fun and exercise.

Cons,
1) A Facebook account is required/isn't required, eventually it will be. The problem isn't so much that an account is required, it what Facebook/Meta does with the information they thoroughly collect about you, me, our friends and relatives, so everybody and how we all relate.

2) Facebook/Meta was never a Operating System company before they bought Oculus. So you end up with updates that screw things up or take features away. For example, when the Quest first came out, you had parties and other communication options in VR, but with an update, Facebook took those things away and required a data vacuum account (Facebook). Another example, the IPD setting got messed up with an update. I then had an IPD pop up in middle of the gaming view regularly. There was an adjustment you could make to disable it but required a sidequest hack every time you used the headset. There is a long thread in a Facebook forum of all the users affected by this but i think Facebook/Meta simply ignored us.

3) They stop providing controllers for your headset. For example, if you own a Quest 1, they stopped selling controllers for it a while back. If it breaks or wears out, you'r SOL. Pretty sad they don't support their customers. They should have a warning to let us know that after you buy the Quest, spare parts will only be available for a limited time. You currently can't replace a Quest 2 controller. Controllers break, things happen. If Facebook/Meta isn't going to allow third parties to develop controllers then Facebook/Meta needs to keep offering them for people who invest into Facebook's products. We all don't have Zuckerberg money, some of us scrape to get our hands on a headset. Mark might want everybody buying a headset every two years, but many people can't afford that.

I've come to the conclusion that Facebook is all about collecting our data and the Quest is a tool to accomplish that goal. I don't believe that they are serious about VR. They bought VR, they didn't develop it. The day another company comes out with a decent wireless VR headset, i can't buy it fast enough.

I rate VR 5 stars, Oculus developed something amazing.
I rate Facebook's/Meta's implementation/purchase of it, 2 stars

Review 1: rating 5.0. Best console ever made to date! There is no learning curve exspecially it you've used an Xbox One. Setup will take a few mainly due to an system update. After that if you have 4K TV you'll want to check your settings to make sure 4K output is selected. reference_output:
rating 5.0. Just as good and in some ways better than the first party controller! Great price on this and the quality is on par with the NES Classic first party controller that comes with the official unit. The buttons and directional pad are super responsive and have a nice satisfying feel to them when pressed. The plastic of the body and buttons feels perfect and I love the design and texture on the face artwork. The start and select buttons are rubber like the original NES controller as well. Overall excellent quality. It's nice that the cable is 6' as well, compared to the way-too-short NES Classic first party controllers short 2' cable.

My only minor issue would be that the A and B buttons are convex instead of concave, but this doesn't affect anything aside from my personal preference for an NES controller.

rating 3.0. PS4: THE GOOD, THE BAD, & THE REALLY BAD! PROS: It has a far less cluttered "desktop" than the XBOX 360's "Dashboard" w/M$'s obsession w/tiles which win 8+ are infected w/! You can play DVD's, & it will even play DVDR's+/-, DVDA's, BR's, & even PAL DVD's too-& plays them all flawlessly, which it wasn't like this when the PS4 first launched in '13! From, what I read, at first PAL DVD's were hit & miss. Not sure about Region coded DVD's/BR's, as I only buy R0 or R1! But it will play PS4 games from regions other than where you live, as I bought Batman: Arkham Knight on here from, a UK based seller & it loaded up & updated just fine-BUT,...from what I read it must have the CUSA encoding on the spine of the case-look it up for more info. The system can be set to automatically update ANY updates for any game as soon as it's done reading the disc! M$ apparently couldn't be bothered w/such "HELPFUL" settings! It has a strong & fast NIC too along w/its CPU & Video card. It has WAY more free content w/regards to avatars & background themes than does the XBOX 360. This is sadly where the pros end!

CONS (minor): I find the controller to be made for smaller child like hands-I guess I'm just used to the XBOX 3260 ones which fit perfectly in your hands, but the PS1 controllers were better as far as that goes. No clue why $ony chose to make them this way, but,..... I also hate the way it "loads" ejects" discs. There is NO TRAY at all! Just an open slot! Like your car CD player. I don't like this at all In fact I hate it! Thanks $ony for taking a page out of M$'s book & thinking that you KNOW what all of your users want/need!

CONS (MAJOR): IT WILL; NOT PLAY ANY CD, STORE BOUGHT OR COPIED, NOR WILL EVEN PLAY MP3 CD'S I BET! IT WILL ALSO NOT PLAY A CD NOR DVD W/VIDEO FILES ON IT-.MPG'S, .AVI'S ETC.! AGAIN,....SERIOUSLY?!?!?!?! WTF?! & YEAH, I STILL BUY CD"s & no I don't own a butter churn, nor am I 60+ y.o.! I have always bought them & always will! Same goes for DVD's & BR's! I will never choose "Digital downloads" of music nor movies over the hard copy of them! If the industry ever stops making them, I will just DL them for FREE & burn them to DISCS myself! & That's a promise! The quality of said compressed files can't ever compare to the original! & if your HD dies, you lost your ENTIRE music & movie collection! BRILLIANT! Then you have to BUY &/or Re-DL it ALL AGAIN!!!!+ HOW lazy do you have to be to NOT just walk over to your machine & pop a disc in? I mean, SERIOUSLY! & some people say it takes up too much space. Do you live in a shoebox? A shed? Give me a freaking break! So, if you're thinking of "upgrading" to a PS4, be prepared to keep that old XBOX-regardless of what gen it is, &/or your old DVD/BR player too, or any CD player you have in case you're not some "digital file obsessed drone" that just buys whatever these corporations cram down your throat, & still buy & listen to CD's! I'd have bought an XBOX 1, if not for the fact that M$ SCREWED me over on replacing my defective unit! & so, I said fine, I'm going to $ony then! & F.U.! I REALLY Wish they hadn't done that, because I'd rather have an XBOX ONE than this! At least I know that the ONE can handle playing EVERYTHING! & I WON'T BE buying a PS5+ unless these issues are SERIOUSLY addressed & changed!

BTW,.......
Speaking of MUSIC-NO PANDORA?! SERIOUSLY?! I don't care for Spotify or whatever other crappy music apps you offer, as they suck compared to PANDORA! SO, I guess it's PANDORA ONLY on my PC then I suppose since M$ doesn't think their users use it either!

persona: This customer prioritizes product readiness for specific use cases, especially backward compatibility, and relies heavily on reviews to make informed decisions; they value time efficiency and are willing to perform technical tasks if the product is not immediately functional, but expect clear, accurate product information upfront.
reference_output: rating 3.0. I was expecting the device to be ready for backwards compatibility but it was not. I bought this drive because my son wanted to play original XBox games.  I had flashed other drives in the past, but I figured I didn't want to waste the time if I can get it ready to go.  Many reviews suggested this HDD was ready, but it was not.  I had to DL the image and tool and flash on the missing sector 2 info.  This was a simple enough operation and the image is less than 300 MB.  I am hoping this becomes part of their build out process before it goes to the customers.  On a related note, I had issues with Kinect games.  I had to pull the drive and DL content and then replace the drive for the game to work.  I have not fully investigated that yet.  If nothing else, my son and I have space and a way to play original XBox games.

persona: This customer values functional, reliable products that deliver on advertised features and work seamlessly across platforms; they prioritize compatibility and performance, are willing to overlook minor flaws if the core experience is strong, and appreciate included accessories and hassle-free setup, but are sensitive to usability limitations like poor controller design or limited social features.
reference_output: rating 4.0. THE must buy for PSVR This game is amazing and so immersive. I love it. It has cross play with Oculus Rift and it works seamlessly. I took off a single star because it isn't easy to add friends via the game and there isn't voice chat during normal games, unless your in squad of people, which in my experience, without a lot of PSN friends, it is hard to set up. This game is a must buy for PSVR fans, and for me would have been a 5 star if voice chat was included during normal game play. Some modes require you to work together and the omission of standard voice chat almost makes it feel like you are fighting smarter AI.

reference_output:
rating 4.0. You get what you pay for Not speaking in terms of value, but in that you are getting a 6-7 hour linear campaign in the vein of the Call of Duty moniker. To think it would be otherwise is basically naivete on the part of the consumer. It's always the populist mindset to root against the 800 pound gorilla in room...Yankees, Patriots, Lakers...and in video games, Call of Duty. But the product they put out is usually pretty consistent and predictable, albeit, they're basically blueprints of prior iterations. I'll just give you some of my perspective of Infinity Ward's versions starting from COD4. I only play single player so I'll give a review on the campaign only. I know, it's shocking to think people still play a game for the single player so to all those who say games like these should solely put out a multiplayer product, they would lose the revenue stream from players like myself.

COD4, in my opinion, was and maybe still is the best fps single-player on the market. It revolutionized the genre when it came out. It was short and linear, but was memorable due to its set-pieces (first time you fell out of the helicopter, the death of a protagonist from a nuclear explosion, the final shot at the end) and innovative missions (sniper, AC-130 and the extra spec op mission). Most games these days, you forget about entirely once you eject the disc. COD4's staying power is due to the fact the game stayed with you long after playing it.

MW2's campaign was a not unexpectedly a flaming turd. It was impossible to surpass its predecessor so they basically tried to shock you by bringing the war to U.S. soil and adding the stupid "No Russian" scene. They killed off so many protagonists that it became laughable and the scene from the Rock was just over the top. Unfortunately, shock value is not lasting and this game was instantly forgettable.

So what to do with the end of the trilogy? Of course you return to what brought you here and that's exactly what this feels like--COD4 re-imagined with parts of MW2. You get some sniper missions, an AC-130 mission, killed off another protagonist, dropped the Eiffel tower (rather flippantly too). Then they peppered in some parts from MW2 like the seal team mission, breaching rooms and the scene from Rio, but it's in another 3rd world country this time. And tried to shock you with civilian casualties again. This sounds like a lot of content within a short time-frame and it really is. I enjoyed it quite a lot and would say if you play a lot of single player games, then this one is right up there. This is probably a better game than COD4, but since it came 4 years after, I docked it a star for lack of originality.

It's a shame that all the 10 year old fanboys came out to review MW3 and Battlefield 3, thus skewing the overall score for each. If you're deciding between the two campaigns, this is the far superior one. (Didn't realize how long this got. Sorry for the length.)

Perfect for smashing some snes games with a ps3 controller tho Review 4: rating 1.0. Warning: LEFT STICK IS NOT ANALOG!!! Very misleading product. To be clear “analog” controls are like steering wheels where it reads the position of the stick and when you let it go it returns to a central position. The left analog stick is NOT analog. It is just like using the up, down, left or right D Pad controls. Review 5: rating 5.0. Great Emulator Controllers! I had been using SNES USB controllers for our emulator PC for a while, however you can only do so much with those when it comes to Gamecube emulation. This was solved with these controllers. The build quality is very solid and they haven't failed yet. Don't let the sub-par reviews deter you. One thing you have to understand is, just like the original PSX analog controllers, the Analog light has to be on for the thumb sticks to work. reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. A Seisure in Game Form If you're looking for a game for the Wii that REALLY shows what the Wii controller can do, look no further - This is it and then some. It's fun, addicting, fast, simple, and is as difficult as you want to take it. You can easily beat the game in an hour or two, but it doesn't end there, that simply unlocks multi-player mode, which is the highlight of the game anyway.

There's a few different multi-player modes, which will have everyone in the room wav... Review 2: rating 2.0. This game was not really my thing -- too frenetic! I read the reviews here and decided that I would give the game a try. I really felt that this was for a much younger audience. I played it for a couple of hours and worked through the mini-games but found myself getting bored. I could DEFINITELY see how this would be a good party game or an ice breaker. I decided to resell my copy and buy something else like Elebits (which I really enjoy).

I cannot pinpoint what bugged... Review 3: rating 2.0. Overpriced and Repetitive WarioWare: Smooth Moves is entertaining, once you get past the HORRIBLE story-mode. They should have ditched it altogether - really shoddy Flash(R) cartoon graphics mixed with a very childish and overly colorful world leads to you being more embarassed when your friends are around than anything else.

The gameplay is pretty good though - you have about 2 seconds to realize what you have to do, and another 3 or 4 to get it done. Very fast paced. Bos... Review 4: rating 4.0. Warioware: Fun Moves I've played it a few times and it was fun, but the kids love it. It's hilarious to watch them play. The games are challenging enough for my 10 year old to enjoy but most are easy enough for my 5 year old to pass the levels. Review 5: rating 4.0. Best In Its Series, Yet Best Of Its Console! This has got to be the best Wii game released yet! Over 200 crazy microgames (205 to be exact) to play! Probably the best Warioware additon so far!

There are only a few...disadvantages that this game has:

1) Lack of Instruction: For every microgame, there are instructions that consist of one or two words and are only shown for a split second before the microgame starts. Most of the microgames have self-explanatory ins... Review 6: rating 3.0. Good game, but not great Some of the minigames are a lot of fun, some are really difficult. The controls can be really sensitive sometimes and sluggish others.
My biggest complaint is you have to complete the single player game to unlock multiplayer. Was a bit of a bummer when we had a few people over and couldn't do multiplayer yet. And once you do unlock it, it's not head to head multiplayer.
I enjoyed Cooking Mama Cook-off a lot more. Review 7: rating 5.0. Great game! Very fun, entertaining, interactive, definitively needs space for jumping, troting, moving, and having lots of fun!!! We all like having a try because it attracts like a magnet all people around. Review 8: rating 4.0. Helps with fine motor skills I am a mom who just got the Wii (mainly because I wanted it). I also have a 5 year old son who hates writing in school because he is not very good with his hand fine motor skills. This game builds his confidence and his motor control. It's very silly and fun and he loves sautéing and shaving. Great family game. I rented it to try it out and HAVE to buy this one. Review 9: rating 5.0. Truly Smooth Though WarioWare: Smooth Moves may not be the deepest of games, it is still by far one of the most entertaining. The game makes very deep and incredible use of the Wiimote's motion sensing capabilities, moreso than even games that are not peppered with motions like WarioWare is.
The game is amazing and provides hours upon hours of entertainment for one to twelve players, a very large range, especially for a video game. Multiplayer is turn-based, and only one Wiimote... Review 10: rating 5.0. Fun and funny This is our family's favorite wii game. It is even fun to watch others play it. But then we all end up jumping up and asking for a turn. THe turns are not so long that the watchers get bored. I'm not sure why it is E10+ and not just E. There isn't anything in the game that is any worse than Bugs Bunny silliness. Review 11: rating 3.0. Good for kids not so much for adults This game is a bunch of short races. Now too much other then that. Kids love it. As an adult I don't get why. They play for hours. Review 12: rating 4.0. Simple review! What you NEED to know This review will be very simple, I will just list a few pros and cons that I feel are essential to know, so I won't bore you with a huge long review and get right to it!

PROS:
Casual Gaming
One of the best casual games I have ever played. Very easy to pick up and play for a minute of 20 if you wanna.

Fun:
Extremely fun! i have played almost all games (203 games total) and they are outrageous and hilarious
... Review 13: rating 5.0. My kids still love this game....after 4 years of playing it! Got this Wario Smooth Moves game for my kiddos 4 years ago. My kids are now 20, 17 and 13...and they still play this game from time to time! It offers quirky little mini games and random puzzles to solve. The graphics are amusing and the music is non-annoying. Overall the game has withstood the test of time very well and my kids have fun playing it, even now. Review 14: rating 3.0. Fun and random One of my favorite games, due to its randomness. A bit disappointing that it is only one player, but still fun. The great thing is it jumps from one thing to the next, different activities, different games, great for those moments when you have 5 minutes before you need to run out the door, and just want a quick-fix of wii! Review 15: rating 5.0. Still my favorite Wii game I love this game. It was one of the first games I ever played on the (at a friend's house). When I got a Wii, this was the first game I got for it. When I loaned it out (stupid) and never saw it again, I bought it again. It's that good. I can see how someone might not like it, but I just love those microgames. It's a great party game too. Review 16: rating 4.0. Good for 6+ We bought this because we thought it would be fun for our son, but I think he was a little to young at the time. Some mini games require a level of accuracy that can be difficult for younger kids. My wife and I love it, and I think he will too once we get him to try it again. Review 17: rating 4.0. Great for parties! This is a very odd game. It's fast paced, and very wacky! I played this game just once at a friends house, and ordered it as soon as I got home! Review 18: rating 2.0. Disappointed. I expected it to be better. I had the WarioWare for the Gamecube and I absolutely love that game. I still play it to this day. It is interesting, strange, and an absolute blast to play with other people. Other than the fact that they made the layout to playing the game more... "attractive," for lack of a better word, I was disappointed in the actual games and the storyline (they repeated Jimmy's story with another guy that looks exactly like him, his story to be... Review 19: rating 5.0. Jimmy P.'s intro is well worth the price of admission I bought this game for my nieces and nephews; they loved it. I knew they would - i had the original WarioWare on the GameBoy Advance. But I had no idea I would have so much fun watching them have so much fun. Some mini-games are easy, some are more confusing/difficult, but they're all fun and a lot of them are hilarious. You will laugh. Now I'm playing it by myself and I LOVE it. I have to beat their high scores to impress them! Review 20: rating 5.0. this is probably one of the most fun games you can play with a group of friends As a gamer with +1,000 games in my collection, this is probably one of the most fun games you can play with a group of friends. If you are a social gamer, YOU NEED THIS. It's one of those Wii games that just about anyone can enjoy.

persona: This customer values engaging multiplayer experiences and high replayability, often drawn to games with strong online communities, loot systems, or competitive modes; they are willing to invest in additional accessories for better gameplay and prioritize game depth and performance over minor technical flaws.
reference_output:
rating 4.0. Good but not great I haven't played a Mario Party game since the Nintendo 64. I never bought the Wii and never wanted too. When I saw this was coming out, I pre-ordered it because I loved the Nintendo 64 ones. I did read up before that you CANNOT use this in handheld mode OR with a Pro Controller. That is disappointing because if you want to play with 4 people, you better have 4 joy-cons. I just purchased a $70 set along with the plastic controller housings so its a bit more comfortable to play. This should be stated on the packaging.

The only other complaint I have so far is only 4 boards. How is it that my N64 games had more boards? And 80 minigames is fine, but why not 100+? This is 2018 and I feel that the games and their content should be vastly improved over games from 15 years ago. I am sure there will be DLC down the road but that's not right to make people fork over money for additional boards, characters, minigames, etc etc. The board you unlock is terrible and the island map is probably the 3rd best. I think the soundtrack could be improved and the maps themselves more lively and interactive.

That being said, this is the formula I remember. 10 coins, buy a star. I'd like to see the cost be 20 coins if you play longer than 10 turns, but with 10 turns you probably only have 4 or 5 stars being bought during the game. I gave this 4 out of 5 despite the previous issues because the game is fun and that's what it's about. Sure more boards would be nice or playing it in handheld mode, but to be honest I only travel with my switch when I am on an airplane so I already set it up on a stand when I'm in the air. The minigames are fun, the graphics are nice, and it's just a fun game to play. I buy games to have fun and as long as it's fun, I'm cool with that.

reference_output:
rating 4.0. Enjoyable in Single Player Yoshi's Crafted World is a platformer that follows the Yoshi formula that we all love- eat enemies, turn them into eggs, and throw them at things! The game can be played solo or two player, but I do not suggest two player as you will frequently run into each other, latch onto the others back, and eat each other. This is goofy fun for a minute but gets annoying after a while and makes completing levels extremely difficult. Solo player works just fine and is fun.

The game opens up with a over world which you choose your destination from. Once you complete the level, the next one will open up, unless you do not have enough flowers (collectible). These flowers and other collectibles are hidden through out each level and are enjoyable to find. Overall platforming is fun as there are plenty of areas to explore and enemies to battle. The game looks great for the switch and runs smooth, I would recommend this game.

The only thing that my original CV1 beats the quest is the integrated sound. You will want a good wireless headset with your quest.

persona: This customer prioritizes functional compatibility and reliable performance when purchasing accessories, particularly for older gaming hardware, and values products that solve specific technical issues without compromising audio balance or usability.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Let's Older Turtle Beach X42 Headset Work, With No Issues --  With the NEW Xbox One Controller (3.55mm input jack on it). Purchased this item to get my old Turtle Beach, X42 headset to work with the new Xbox One controller, with the 3.5mm input.  I tried several other 2.5mm to 3.5mm adapters and NONE of them worked.  This item has a 2.5mm input, for the older 2.5mm, male plug on the X42.  The end that plugs into the controller has the 3.5mm plug, which plugs into the new Xbox One controller, with 3.5mm input hole.  Both chat and game sounds work normally!!  It works straight out of the box, no fuss, no issues.  If you have the older Xbox controller, you will need to get the stereo adapter for the controller, and test that set-up out.
